free_text: Gamma-ray production cross sections from neutron
interaction with iron
WNR LANSCE accelerator.
800 MeV protons on tungsten target.
607.9 g natural iron disk, 99.9% pure, oriented at
45 degrees to neutron beam.
High purity germanium gamma detector at 125
degrees.
Neutron flux measured using fission ion
chamber containing thin deposits of both 238U and
Detector efficiency measured using a set of Amersham
source, a calibrated 152Eu source, and a 56Co source.
Neutron fluence determined using ENDF/B-VI cross
sections for for 238U and 235U fission at neutron
energy below 16 MeV, and for higher energies, data of
Lisowski et al., Juelich Conf. (1991) p. 732.
Corrected for neutron attenuation, multiple scattering,
and gamma attenuation up to 20 MeV, using Monte Carlo
code MCNPX. Above 20 MeV, corrections made using
estimates of neutron production, average path length,
and cross section for secondary neutrons (these
corrections tend to be large and less reliable).
Corrected for solid angle of sample with respect to
beam (+9%).
Statistical uncertainties given.
VM
DR Line 298 in subent 2 corrected
BP: Corrected reaction information, added
status.
Data from authors.
Data from a later experiment.
